title: Diplazium iridiphyllum

The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Diplazium iridiphyllum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can also be found in modern herbal preparations such as capsules and extracts.
